title: selliguea pentaphylla
intro: Selliguea pentaphylla, also known as the five-leaved polypody or polypodium, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia. It is a medicinal plant that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines and other Southeast Asian countries, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Selliguea pentaphylla include reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
